config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan atas nama kepentingan masyarakat secara umum, karena justru hak itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan dan mayoritas yang semena-mena. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang kuat melindungi individu, bukan yang mudah menginjaknya demi alasan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that idea makes me uneasy because history shows how easily “the common good” can be used to excuse censorship, discrimination, or abuse by the majority. Society absolutely has legitimate interests in public safety, health, and order, but those goals should be pursued within firm limits that protect individual rights. To me, a healthy society is one that treats individual rights as the baseline, not as something to be overridden whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum, karena di Indonesia sendiri kita sering melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil. Kepentingan publik memang penting, terutama untuk keamanan, kesehatan, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu tidak boleh jadi alasan untuk mengabaikan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum tiap orang. Kalau hak individu dibuat terlalu lemah, yang paling diuntungkan biasanya justru mayoritas atau pihak berkuasa, bukan masyarakat secara keseluruhan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang bisa menyeimbangkan kepentingan umum tanpa mengorbankan hak dasar warganya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that usually becomes a blank check for majorities or governments to trample unpopular people. The argument for the common good is real, but history here makes me wary of letting it override free speech, due process, or equal protection unless there is a very clear and limited necessity. In my view, society works best when individual rights are the default and any restriction has to be narrowly justified, not assumed. If we weaken that standard, we do not get a healthier society; we just make abuse easier.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, ketidakadilan, atau perlakuan semena-mena terhadap orang yang lemah atau tidak populer. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, karena tanpa perlindungan itu yang muncul bukan kebaikan bersama melainkan kekuasaan mayoritas atau elite. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling serius melindungi martabat dan kebebasan setiap warganya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that idea is dangerous because it too easily turns into “the majority gets to decide,” and history here shows how that can lead to censorship, discrimination, and abuse. I do think society has real claims in areas like public safety, health, and emergency response, but those claims should be narrow, necessary, and accountable rather than treated as a default trump card. If individual rights are not the baseline, then the people who end up paying the price are usually the unpopular, the powerless, or the inconvenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
